LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, AMENDED DEMURRER. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. NO FACTS ALLEGED THAT DEFENDANT OWED A DUTY. GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 815 STATES THAT DIRECT TORT LIABILITY OF PUBLIC ENTITIES MUST BE BASED ON A SPECIFIC STATUTE DECLARING THEM TO BE LIABLE, OR AT LEAST CREATING SOME SPECIFIC DUTY OF CARE. SEE EASTBURN VS. REGIONAL FIRE PROTECTION AUTHORITY (2003) 31 CAL. 4TH 1175, AND MUNOZ VS. CITY OF UNION CITY (2004) 120 CAL.APP.4TH 1077. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

AMENDED DEMURRER
Off Calendar Jun-30-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, AMENDED DEMURRER. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer is sustained with ten days leave to amend. No facts alleged that defendant owed a duty. Government Code section 815 states that direct tort liability of public entities must be based on a specific statute declaring them to be liable, or at least creating some specific duty of care. See Eastburn vs. Regional Fire Protection Authority (2003) 31 Cal. 4th 1175, and Munoz vs. City of Union City (2004) 120 Cal.App.4th 1077. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
